creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2011-02-05 14:52, Dimitry Andric wrote:
>> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/gauche-0.9.log
...
> There seems to be something fishy going on in its internal hash tables,
> or maybe with the Boehm garbage collector it is using.
...
>> http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.9-exp.20110113095120/sather-1.2.2_2.log
...
> There is definitely something fishy with boehm-gc... :(

Ok, I have identified some problems with devel/boehm-gc, and the other
boehm-gc ports, which also apply to the private copy of boehm-gc that
lang/gauche uses.  If there are any other ports that use private copies
of boehm-gc, please let me know. :)

The first problem is that boehm-gc uses a signal handler to catch write
accesses to read-only memory areas, so it can unprotect them when
needed.  However, the signal handler only checks the si_code field for
BUS_PAGE_FAULT (12), which was valid for FreeBSD 6.x and earlier.

 From 7.0 onwards, by default this type of protection fault is returned
via SIGSEGV instead of SIGBUS, and the si_code field is set to
SEGV_ACCERR (2) in this case.  (At least normally, there is a sysctl to
revert to the old behaviour.)

In this upstream change, the logic was corrected:
http://bdwgc.cvs.sourceforge.net/viewvc/bdwgc/bdwgc/os_dep.c?r1=1.88&r2=1.89

I chose to also add a check for the correct SIGBUS or SIGSEGV, which
seems appropriate; I have sent this upstream too.

The second problem is that in dyn_load.c dl_iterate_phdr() is marked
with #pragma weak, even on FreeBSD 7.0 and later, where it is available.
This only turns out to be a problem when using newer binutils, leading
to a crash in one of boehm-gc's tests.

This was fixed upstream in this change:
http://bdwgc.cvs.sourceforge.net/viewvc/bdwgc/bdwgc/dyn_load.c?r1=1.23&r2=1.24

I have added both changes to the boehm-gc, boehm-gc-redirect and
boehm-gc-threaded ports, and added all the other patches for boehm-gc to
lang/gauche's private copy too.

This makes all those ports build and test succesfully on i386 and amd64,
with binutils 2.17.
